title: Swimmer's ear
license_notes: Use as public patient-education source support. Avoid verbatim copying into primitives unless reuse terms are confirmed for the exact content.
supports: The medical term for swimmer's ear is otitis externa.
Symptoms can include ear pain, itching, drainage, and tenderness.
Treatment may include clinician-directed ear drops.
Fever or pain and redness behind the ear require medical evaluation.
